title: Rocket Lab delays first Neutron launch to 2026
text: Rocket Lab has delayed the first launch of its Neutron medium-lift rocket to 2026, according to statements made during the company's Q3 2025 earnings call. CEO Peter Beck indicated the first flight is now expected in the fourth quarter of 2026. The delay reflects the complex development challenges inherent in creating a new medium-lift launch vehicle. Rocket Lab has made significant progress on Neutron development, with the vehicle passing critical testing milestones throughout 2025. The company has opened Launch Complex 3 at Wallops to support Neutron operations and expects the first flight hardware to arrive at the launch facility in Q1 2026, pending qualification testing. Despite the delay, Beck expressed confidence in the timeline, noting that the schedule is tight with no margin for error.
